note: Views AJAX History has no settings form; it is configured per view via a Views display extender and ships a JS library that hooks Views AJAX into the History API. Cases are `recipe` (response-graded): they probe documented facts an agent would otherwise read source for. No site mutation.

evals.prompt: On a Drupal 11 site, what must already be true about a view before Views AJAX History can push filter/pager state to the browser URL, and where do I turn the feature on?
evals.expected_output: The view's 'Use AJAX' option must be enabled; then tick the 'AJAX history' checkbox that appears in the Use AJAX dialog under Advanced. hook_views_pre_render only attaches when $view->ajaxEnabled() is true.

evals.prompt: Which kind of Views plugin does Views AJAX History provide to add its per-view options, and what is the plugin id?
evals.expected_output: A Views display extender plugin with id 'ajax_history' (class AjaxHistory), registered in views.settings display_extenders on install.

evals.prompt: What are the two configuration keys Views AJAX History stores per view display, and what does each do?
evals.expected_output: enable_history (boolean) turns history on for the display; exclude_args (text, newline-separated) lists query-key prefixes to strip from the pushed URL. Stored under display_extenders.ajax_history and schema-defined.

evals.prompt: Once enabled, how does Views AJAX History make the browser back/forward buttons and bookmarking work for an AJAX view?
evals.expected_output: It calls history.pushState() with a cleaned URL each time a filter/pager changes (so the URL is bookmarkable/shareable) and binds a popstate handler (loadView) that re-issues the Views AJAX request on back/forward.

evals.prompt: Which asset library does Views AJAX History attach to an AJAX view, and which core Drupal.Ajax methods does its JavaScript override?
evals.expected_output: The views_ajax_history/history library (js/views_ajax_history.js). It overrides Drupal.Ajax.prototype beforeSerialize (pager clicks), beforeSubmit (exposed-form submits), and beforeSend (AJAX URL cleanup).

evals.prompt: I want certain query parameters kept out of the URL that Views AJAX History writes to browser history. Which per-view option controls this and how are the entries matched?
evals.expected_output: The 'Exclude query arguments from the URL' option (exclude_args): one query key per line, each matched with a 'starts with' comparison and removed from the pushed URL (passed to JS as excludeArgs).

evals.prompt: What is the only Drupal module dependency of Views AJAX History, and does it add an admin settings form or any permissions?
evals.expected_output: It depends only on core Views (drupal:views). It has no site-wide settings form (configure is null) and provides no permissions or Drush commands; configuration is per view via the display extender.

evals.prompt: When a user applies a new exposed filter on an AJAX view with history enabled, what does the module do with the pager 'page' parameter in the URL?
evals.expected_output: In beforeSubmit the module removes the 'page' query parameter so a newly applied filter returns fresh results from the first page rather than an out-of-range page.
